plan_review_notes: Approve the safe surface-cleanup slice: prefer sisyphus in CLI/help and generated guidance, clarify taskflow.mcp_server as a compatibility launcher path, and keep high-risk identifiers out of scope.

plan_review_history.notes: Revised the task from planning-only into the first implementation slice of the naming migration: update user-facing CLI/help text, generated verification mappings, and documentation around compatibility launcher paths while explicitly leaving package/config/protocol identifiers unchanged.
Approve the safe surface-cleanup slice: prefer sisyphus in CLI/help and generated guidance, clarify taskflow.mcp_server as a compatibility launcher path, and keep high-risk identifiers out of scope.

spec_review_notes: Freeze the implementation scope to safe user-facing naming cleanup only: CLI prog string, generated task-doc wording, and compatibility-path documentation. Do not rename src/taskflow, .taskflow.toml, taskflow.event.v1, or TF task ids in this task.

normal_cases.name: CLI help surfaces `sisyphus` as the program name.
Generated feature and issue plan text uses `sisyphus verify`.

edge_cases.name: `taskflow` remains a working runtime alias even though the help and generated guidance prefer `sisyphus`.
MCP docs remain technically accurate by describing `taskflow.mcp_server` as a compatibility path rather than renaming it prematurely.

exception_cases.name: This slice does not accidentally rename high-risk identifiers such as `.taskflow.toml`, `taskflow.event.v1`, or `src/taskflow/`.
No docs instruct users to run commands that do not exist in the current codebase.

verification_methods.target: CLI help surfaces sisyphus as the program name.
Generated feature and issue plan text uses sisyphus verify.
taskflow remains a working runtime alias and MCP docs stay accurate.
verification_methods.method: manual review of updated docs and unchanged compatibility entry points

verification_methods.target: High-risk identifiers are not renamed accidentally.
verification_methods.method: manual review of diff for config, event schema, and package paths
